parsed_review.summary:
CP00-881 (RP29 Commercial Readiness — remaining P03 interface evidence/review/handoff rows + P04 commercial UI foundation; RP29.P03.M06.S12-RP29.P04.M04.S15; Risk C; 150 units) passes independent read-only review. Inspection was done via Read/Grep/Glob over source (summary-only correctly disallowed for this pack); no Bash, no writes.

Verified support for the descriptor-only/production_ready claim: (1) Implementation is descriptor-only end-to-end. states.js, model.js, policies.js, service.js, interface.js, the new ui.js, and registry section rows all carry explicit runtime_execution:false, writes_product_state:false, permission_decision_written:false, audit_event_written:false, runtime_receipt_emitted:false, real_client_data_included:false, credential_or_secret_included:false attestations, deep-frozen. A targeted grep over the full generated contract returned 0 positive occurrences of any runtime/write/real-data flag. (2) Coverage math is exact: COMMERCIAL_INTERFACE_ROWS=20, COMMERCIAL_UI_ROWS=22, so required_section_rows sum to 9+20+20+20+8+8+8+20+22+15 = 150; P03=77, P04=73; deliverable distribution {test:13,hermes_evidence:6,claude_review:11,implementation:52,contract:15,security_audit:14,ui:37,fixture:2} and micro_title counts match the pack baseline and COMMERCIAL_CP881_REQUIREMENTS exactly. (3) Validators (validators.js: validateCommercialInterfaceContract, validateCommercialUiSurfaceMatrix, validateCommercialCp881...Coverage/Descriptor, validateCommercialReadinessContract) rigorously enforce distribution/row drift, authority boundary, per-row no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data guards, fail-closed denied/cross-tenant behavior, UI no-unauthorized-count-leak, permission-badge/audit-hint no-write, build-smoke no-runtime, Hermes no-receipt, Claude read-only, model registry, capabilities and safety gates; the validate-rp29 script chains the full CP00-873→CP00-881 binding and re-runs coverage/descriptor/contract validators. (4) Tests in model.test.js and service.test.js assert exactly these invariants for CP00-881 (binding, coverage, descriptor runtime-closed, UI no-leak boundaries, contract projection current, compatibility alias). (5) No real data or secrets — only synthetic tenants (tenant-synthetic-alpha/beta); every credential/secret/audit hit is a negative attestation or guard name. (6) Boundaries intact: Claude is read-only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, review_required_state routes to human review); Hermes is control-plane evidence only (gate H29, emits_runtime_receipt:false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false), not product runtime; human approval required for runtime opening, deferred to CP00-882; local validation does not claim enterprise trust. (7) Generated commercial-readiness-contract.json is the deterministic registry projection with current_pack/latest_pack CP00-881, latest_projection CommercialCp881InterfaceEvidenceUiFoundationDescriptor, mandatory_artifacts including packages/commercial/src/ui.js, validation.valid:true. (8) Scope hygiene: changed-file-scope.json isolates active pack files from unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs), and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (the new ui.js is included).

Review-hardening posture (CP178+) is sound. The prior pack-level review attempt (attempt-01) terminated with an API socket error and was correctly normalized to status invalid_review / valid_review:false / closeout_eligible:false with parsed_review:null — i.e., auth/CLI-error output was refused as valid evidence, exactly as required. This review supersedes attempt-01; the readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, well-formed JSON). production_ready:false and the pending command-evidence/construction-inspection/adjudication/claude-review-result are the correct expected pre-closeout values for a descriptor-only pack; no production_ready gate is weakened. blocks_goal_closeout is false: this pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim, so the human/enterprise-trust approval authority boundary lies outside this receipt.

No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. The PASS attests that the implementation, contracts, tests, and boundaries support closeout under read-only inspection; final closeout remains contingent on the deterministic validation ladder (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) and receipt/adjudication normalization, which are the normal post-review steps and are not review blockers.
